problem.title: KISS-ICP throughput and drift trade-off on the second public HDL-400 reference window
problem.question: Which KISS-ICP odometry profile should stay as the current default on a deeper HDL-400 public reference set while keeping faster and denser alternatives comparable?

dataset.reference_role: public reference trajectory sliced from the HDL-400 pose trace export

variants.label: Balanced local map
variants.intent: Keep the current repository default as the odometry baseline.

variants.label: Fast recent map
variants.intent: Reduce source density and map pressure to improve FPS.

variants.label: Dense local map
variants.intent: Increase source density and local-map richness to fight odometry drift.
